ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic latte entry; no specific code exists for butterfly pea or taro-flavoured iced latte. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific code for purple/butterfly-pea iced latte. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specialty flavoured iced latte product.

main_ingredients: butterfly pea flower extract or taro

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard takeaway cup of iced purple gold latte, approximately 350 ml, as served at EC Coffee Bar.

nutrient_source.title: Cafe Latte with Whole Milk — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with butterfly pea flower beverage composition estimates from peer-reviewed literature (Terahara et al., 2004; Palamthodi & Lele, 2019)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / peer-reviewed literature
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Purple Gold Latte' exists in any approved primary database. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central cafe latte with whole milk (FDC ID 174032) as the closest structural analogue (espresso + whole milk base), adjusted for the addition of butterfly pea flower extract (negligible macronutrient contribution) and a moderate sugar syrup addition typical of specialty cafe drinks in Singapore. Serving size estimated at 350 g (approximately 350 ml). Per-100g values derived by scaling from the adapted composite. Butterfly pea flower extract contributes anthocyanins but negligible protein, fat, or carbohydrate at typical use levels.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of milk and dairy-based beverages; latte with whole milk analogous estimate — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Purple Gold Latte. GI estimated at 41 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for whole milk latte / cafe latte (GI approximately 38–45 range for milk-dominant beverages with moderate added sugar). Butterfly pea flower extract does not materially alter the glycemic response. Added sugar syrup raises GI slightly above plain milk. GL per serving = (41 × 7.8 g carbohydrate per 100g × 350g serving / 100) / 100 = approximately 11.3. gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~147 mg per 350 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single serving of this iced latte contributes approximately 147 mg sodium, which is within acceptable limits for most hypertensive individuals following a low-sodium diet (target <2300 mg/day), but added sugar content should be monitored if consumed daily.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar syrup contributing approximately 25 g sugar per 350 ml serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: The added sugar syrup in this specialty latte raises the glycemic load to approximately 11 per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request reduced or no sugar syrup and limit consumption to occasional intake to avoid postprandial glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from whole milk (~5.3 g per 350 ml serving) and presence of anthocyanins from butterfly pea flower
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content from whole milk is moderate; individuals with cardiovascular disease may benefit from requesting a low-fat or plant-based milk alternative, while the butterfly pea flower anthocyanins may offer mild antioxidant benefit, though clinical evidence remains preliminary.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Purple Gold Latte (Iced) is a specialty cold coffee beverage served at EC Coffee Bar on Carpenter Street, Singapore, and is characteristic of the contemporary Malaysian-Singaporean artisan cafe scene. The drink is built on an espresso or strong coffee base combined with whole milk and a distinctive purple colouring derived from butterfly pea flower (Clitoria ternatea) extract, which imparts a vivid violet-to-indigo hue. A sugar syrup is added for sweetness, and the beverage is served cold over ice, yielding a refreshing, mildly sweet, and creamy drink with subtle floral and earthy notes from the butterfly pea flower. The 'gold' element may refer to a golden turmeric or honey accent layer, depending on the specific recipe. Per 100 g, the drink provides approximately 62 kcal, 2.8 g protein, 2.4 g fat, and 7.8 g carbohydrate, primarily from milk and added sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at 41 (low), making it a moderate glycemic-load beverage at a standard 350 ml serving. Clinically, the primary concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes is the added sugar content; those with hypertension should note the moderate sodium from milk. Butterfly pea flower anthocyanins are associated with antioxidant properties in preliminary research. This beverage is vegetarian and halal-suitable. It is priced at SGD 10.40 at EC Coffee Bar.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Purple Gold Latte?
Is butterfly pea flower latte good for diabetics?
Purple Gold Latte EC Coffee Bar Singapore
Purple Gold Latte GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Purple Gold Latte exists in any approved primary nutrient database (HPB Foodo, USDA, IMR Malaysia MyFCD). Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central cafe latte with whole milk (FDC ID 174032) as the closest structural analogue, with adjustments for butterfly pea flower extract and added sugar syrup. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using milk-based latte analogues. Confidence is medium due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted estimates based on USDA FoodData Central cafe latte with whole milk (FDC ID 174032); no exact approved-source entry exists for this specific product. 2. Butterfly pea flower extract contributes negligible macronutrients at typical use levels but provides anthocyanin pigments. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA source for this milk-based beverage category; naturally zero by product formulation at this scale. 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero for a liquid milk-coffee beverage with no fibre-contributing solids. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero in a milk-coffee beverage; butterfly pea flower extract at typical concentrations contributes negligible ascorbic acid. 6. GI set to 41 as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database milk-based latte entries; gi_category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold rule. 7. GL per serving calculated as (41 × 7.8 / 100) × 350 / 100 = 11.3. 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are all estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 174032 (cafe latte, whole milk) and are not -1 sentinel values as the source provides these data. 9. Cuisine tagged as Malaysian per input; the drink is served in Singapore at a Malaysian-style coffee bar. 10. Price SGD 10.40 per input record from field survey data. 11. Halal status assumed based on ingredients (no pork, no alcohol); not independently verified from a certification body.
